section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a positive straight leg raise.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Straight leg raise is a physical exam maneuver and its result is an observed finding, not a subjective symptom, so it belongs in the Objective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor appreciates a 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a physical exam finding and is correctly placed in the Objective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's right strength is 4 out of 5.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an exam finding of motor strength and is correctly placed in the Objective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's left strength is 5 out of 5.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an exam finding of motor strength and is correctly placed in the Objective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is currently taking metformin.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Current medications the patient is already taking belong in the Subjective section unless framed as a treatment change or order.
Use of prior or current medications reported by the patient is subjective history and should be in the Subjective section.
Use of prior or current medications reported by the patient is subjective history and should be in the Subjective section.
Self-directed prior treatments are part of the patient's subjective history and belong in the Subjective section.
Self-directed prior treatments are part of the patient's subjective history and belong in the Subjective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor assesses the patient has an acute lumbar strain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a diagnostic assessment and is correctly placed in the Assessment section.
This is an active problem/diagnosis and is correctly placed in the Assessment section.
This is being used as a problem/diagnosis summary and is acceptable in the Assessment section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor ordered a refill of lisinopril 20 milligrams once a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a medication order/change and is correctly placed in the Plan section.
This is a referral/order and is correctly placed in the Plan section.
This is a diagnostic order and is correctly placed in the Plan section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor wants to see the patient in a couple weeks.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a follow-up plan and is correctly placed in the Plan section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a new prescription and is correctly placed in the Plan section.
